name: Botulinum Toxin
description: Most toxic substance known; blocks acetylcholine release at neuromuscular junction

name: Tetrodotoxin
description: Sodium channel blocker 1200x more toxic than cyanide; found in fugu

name: Ricin
description: Protein synthesis inhibitor; easily extracted from castor beans; bioweapon concern

name: Abrin
description: Ribosome-inactivating protein; single seed can kill; bright red and black seed

name: VX Nerve Agent
description: Most potent nerve agent; persistent oily liquid; inhibits acetylcholinesterase

name: Sarin (GB)
description: Volatile nerve agent; used in Tokyo subway attack (1995); rapid onset

name: Soman (GD)
description: Nerve agent that ages rapidly making treatment difficult after exposure

name: Tabun (GA)
description: First nerve agent developed (1936); fruity odor; less potent than sarin

name: Amatoxin (alpha-amanitin)
symptoms: Delayed GI symptoms|liver failure|kidney failure|death in 6-16 days
description: Responsible for 90% of mushroom poisoning deaths; inhibits RNA polymerase II

name: Muscarine
description: Parasympathomimetic toxin; namesake of muscarinic acetylcholine receptors

name: Strychnine
antidote: Benzodiazepines for seizures; no specific antidote
description: Glycine receptor antagonist; formerly used as rat poison; extremely painful death

name: Aconitine
antidote: No specific antidote; amiodarone for arrhythmia
description: Ancient arrow poison; sodium channel activator; queen of poisons in ancient Rome

name: Coniine
symptoms: Ascending paralysis|respiratory failure|death with mind intact
description: Killed Socrates; nicotinic receptor antagonist; causes ascending paralysis

name: Oleandrin
description: Cardiac glycoside; all parts of plant toxic; smoke from burning is dangerous

name: Taxine
antidote: No antidote; CPR and cardiac support
description: Cardiotoxic alkaloid; ancient arrow poison; all parts except red aril are lethal

name: Hippomane Toxins
symptoms: Severe skin blistering|blindness if sap contacts eyes|GI hemorrhage
description: Most dangerous tree; rainwater runoff causes burns; smoke causes blindness

name: Solanine
description: Glycoalkaloid in green/sprouted potatoes and nightshade berries

name: Atropine (toxic dose)
description: Anticholinergic; medicinal at low doses but poisonous from plant ingestion

name: D-Tubocurarine
description: South American arrow poison; blocks nicotinic receptors; basis of modern anesthesia

name: Taipan Venom
description: Most toxic snake venom; single bite can kill 100 adults; neurotoxic and hemotoxic

name: King Cobra Venom
description: Largest venomous snake; neurotoxin causes respiratory paralysis

name: Black Mamba Venom
symptoms: Metallic taste|blurred vision|paralysis|respiratory failure|death in hours
description: Fastest snake; dendrotoxins block potassium channels; 100% fatal untreated

name: Box Jellyfish Venom
symptoms: Extreme pain|cardiac arrest|skin necrosis|death in minutes
description: Most venomous marine animal; death possible within 2-5 minutes

name: Blue-Ringed Octopus Venom
antidote: No antivenom; CPR and artificial ventilation
description: Contains tetrodotoxin; painless bite; paralysis with awareness; fatal without ventilation

name: Conotoxin
description: Contains hundreds of unique peptide toxins; some species lethal to humans

name: Deathstalker Venom
description: Most dangerous scorpion; neurotoxic peptides; potentially fatal to children

name: Loxoscelism
antidote: No antivenom in US; wound care and dapsone
description: Dermonecrotic venom; skin lesion can grow for weeks; rarely fatal

name: Atracotoxin
description: Most dangerous spider to humans; males more venomous than females

name: Sulfur Mustard (HD)
antidote: No antidote; decontamination and supportive care
description: WWI chemical weapon; vesicant; effects delayed 2-24 hours; carcinogenic

name: Lewisite
description: Arsenic-based chemical weapon; unlike mustard gas causes immediate pain

name: Chlorine Gas
description: First modern chemical weapon (WWI Ypres 1915); heavier than air

name: Phosgene
symptoms: Fresh hay smell|delayed pulmonary edema 24-48 hours|death
antidote: No antidote; immediate rest and oxygen therapy
description: Responsible for 80% of WWI chemical deaths; insidious delayed action

name: Hydrogen Cyanide
antidote: Hydroxocobalamin or sodium thiosulfate + amyl nitrite
description: Blocks cytochrome c oxidase; prevents cellular oxygen use; rapid death

name: Arsenic Trioxide
description: King of poisons; accumulates in hair and nails; chronic exposure causes cancer

name: Thallium Sulfate
symptoms: Hair loss|peripheral neuropathy|organ failure|death over weeks
description: Odorless tasteless poison; slow onset made it popular for homicide

name: Lead
description: Cumulative neurotoxin; especially harmful to children; no safe blood level

name: Mercury (methylmercury)
description: Bioaccumulates in fish; crosses blood-brain barrier; Minamata disease

name: Asbestos Fibers
antidote: No treatment for fiber removal; cancer therapy
description: Microscopic mineral fibers; 20-50 year latency for cancer; banned in many countries

name: Ionizing Radiation (acute)
symptoms: Nausea|vomiting|hair loss|bone marrow failure|death (>6 Gy lethal)
description: Acute radiation syndrome; cell death from DNA damage; lethal above 6 Gray whole body

name: Parathion
description: Extremely toxic insecticide; banned in many countries; inhibits cholinesterase

name: DDT (dichlorodiphenyltrichloroethane)
description: Persistent organic pollutant; bioaccumulates; banned for agriculture use

name: Paraquat
antidote: No effective antidote; Fullers earth if early
description: Extremely toxic herbicide; produces superoxide radicals; delayed lung fibrosis

name: Glyphosate
description: Most-used herbicide worldwide; low acute toxicity; debated chronic effects

name: Warfarin (rodenticide dose)
antidote: Vitamin K1 injection + fresh frozen plasma
description: Anticoagulant rat poison; also used as blood thinner medication at lower doses

name: Brodifacoum
symptoms: Severe internal bleeding|hemorrhage from all orifices|death
description: Superwarfarin 100x more potent; single-feed kill; very long half-life (>20 days)

name: Tetanospasmin
description: Second most toxic substance known; blocks inhibitory neurotransmitter release

name: Diphtheria Toxin
description: Inhibits protein synthesis via ADP-ribosylation of EF-2; vaccine-preventable

name: Shiga Toxin
antidote: No antitoxin; supportive care and hydration
description: Ribosome-inactivating protein; causes hemolytic uremic syndrome in children

name: Conotoxin (geography cone)
description: Most venomous snail; multiple toxin cocktail; painless sting can be lethal

name: Palytoxin
description: One of most toxic non-protein substances; disrupts ion channels; aquarium risk

name: Batrachotoxin
description: Steroidal alkaloid; indigenous peoples use for blow darts; irreversible Na channel opener

name: Saxitoxin
description: Paralytic shellfish poisoning; 1000x more toxic than cyanide; sodium channel blocker
